_comment: Objective gates for the Veo 3.1 extend-chain render (BOT-030). The episode.mp4 visual expectations are graded by the grader SAMPLING KEYFRAMES of the actual MP4 (media-judge) - never from filenames or text. Structural expectations are graded from the ffprobe output recorded in summary.md. Eval 1 supplies a complete continuous-plan.md so the skill has a real input (a base frame is generated from the plan; no external bible images are needed - the base frame IS generated here, unlike the Kling/Seedance siblings). Eval 2 omits the plan to prove the clean-failure path. NEVER hand-code regex - the grader judges the artifacts. The defining mechanic the summary must disclose honestly: extend-video RETURNS THE FULL grown video each hop, so there is NO concat, and the audio is NATIVE Veo audio (NOT an added bed).

evals.prompt: Render the continuous shot for the project robot-meadow from its continuous-plan.md. Use the defaults.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/robot-meadow/episode.mp4 — ONE continuous shot built as a Veo 3.1 image-to-video base (generated from a single nano-banana-pro base frame of the planned character) extended by extend-video hops, each hop returning the FULL grown video so there is NO concat, with native Veo audio throughout, in which the SAME character is held across the whole take with no hard cut. ffprobe shows a video stream and an audio stream and a duration greater than the base clip. Plus artifacts/robot-meadow/summary.md recording the base-frame model, the Veo base slug, each extend hop and its beat, the final continuous duration, the native-audio note, the NO-concat note, and the cost basis (ai-gen estimate).

files.content:
# Continuous plan: robot-meadow

One continuous cinematic shot, 3D-animated Pixar-style, bright cheerful color grading, soft warm morning light, shallow depth of field, polished render.
Character (frozen tokens): a small round friendly cartoon robot, glossy white and warm-orange rounded body, one big glowing cyan eye on a smooth screen face — maintain the EXACT same character identity for the whole shot.
Base scene: a sunlit green meadow with wildflowers under a bright blue sky, a playful adventure.
Base image: the little robot stands in the sunlit meadow among wildflowers, blinking its big cyan eye, morning light and floating pollen.
Base motion: wide establishing shot, gentle push-in, the little robot wakes up and stretches in the meadow, blinking its big cyan eye, morning light and floating pollen.
Hop 1: the camera continues to glide forward as the robot turns and chases a glowing butterfly through the wildflowers, stubby legs pumping, joyful, petals scattering.
Hop 2: the robot slows and lies back in the soft grass looking up in wonder as the butterfly lands gently on its eye-screen, a warm happy moment.
Aspect-ratio: 16:9. Total: about 22s (8s base + two 7s hops). Audio: whimsical playful orchestral score, gentle nature ambience, soft robotic chirps and a happy beep.
One continuous take, no cut, smooth continuous motion, stable picture, no flicker, no identity drift.

evals._note: No external bible PNGs are required for this skill - the ONE base start frame is generated by nano-banana-pro FROM the plan's base-image prompt + frozen character tokens, then the Veo base i2v + extend chain runs off that. Test wiring need only materialize continuous-plan.md.
expectations: artifacts/robot-meadow/episode.mp4 exists and is ONE coherent continuous shot: the SAME character (the friendly glossy white-and-orange one-cyan-eye robot from the plan) is recognizable from the first sampled keyframe to the last - no identity drift and no hard cut between the base and the extensions (verified by sampling the video keyframes)

the sampled keyframes show the plan executed as a single continuous take that develops over time - a wake-up in the meadow, then a chase of the butterfly, then the butterfly landing - reading as one unbroken shot that progresses, not a static clip and not separate stitched scenes (verified by sampling the video keyframes)

ffprobe of episode.mp4 shows BOTH a video stream and an audio stream present, and a format duration GREATER than the base clip duration (every successful extend grows the whole video) - the verification output is recorded in summary.md

summary.md records the base-frame model (fal-ai/nano-banana-pro, or the image-chain fallback that produced it), the Veo base slug (fal-ai/veo3.1/image-to-video), and each extend hop with its slug (fal-ai/veo3.1/extend-video) and its continuation beat, plus a cost basis derived from ai-gen estimate (NOT the JSON credits_used field)

summary.md states clearly that there is NO concat - extend-video returns the WHOLE grown video each hop - and that the audio is NATIVE Veo audio (NOT an added room-tone bed), and carries the one-line architecture note that this is an image-to-video base plus an extend chain, a different architecture from Seedance's single-pass reference-to-video and Kling's per-shot i2v plus ffmpeg concat, scored head-to-head in the KB results-log

evals.prompt: Render the continuous shot for the project no-plan. The continuous-plan.md is missing.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that the required continuous-plan.md does not exist, records a clean failure in state.md naming the missing required input and routing back to phase 1 (continuous-plan), and produces no episode.mp4 and no fabricated/placeholder MP4.
expectations: no episode.mp4 is produced for artifacts/no-plan/ (the render conditions on the continuous-plan and no MP4 was fabricated in its absence)

the missing required input (continuous-plan.md) is recorded as a clean failure - state.md is marked blocked, names the missing file, and routes back to phase 1 (continuous-plan) - rather than silently skipped, worked around, or rendered without the plan
